div1.entryFree:
Gentry, 1) rank by birth: she conjures him by knighthood, g. Lucr. 569. thou shouldst not alter the article of thy g. Wiv. II, 1, 53. which no less adorns our g. than our parents' noble names, Wint. I, 2, 393. exempt from ancient g. H6A II, 4, 93. g., title, wisdom, Cor. III, 1, 144.
2) the class of gentlemen, the people of good birth and breeding: All's I, 2, 16. John V, 2, 31. H8 I, 1, 76. Cor. II, 1, 254. Mcb. V, 2, 9. Cymb. III, 7, 7. V, 1, 18. V, 2, 8.
3) courtesy: to show us so much g. and good will, Hml. II, 2, 22. he is the card and calendar of g. V, 2, 114.
